title: Finding Eigenvalues using Power Method
description: Algorithm to find dominant eigenvalue of a matrix
code:
POWER_METHOD(A, max_iterations):
 # Initialize random vector
 v = random_vector(n)
 
 for i in range(max_iterations):
 # Multiply by matrix
 w = A @ v
 
 # Normalize to prevent overflow
 eigenvalue = max(abs(w))
 v = w / eigenvalue
 
 return eigenvalue, v
 # Dominant eigenvalue and corresponding eigenvector
explanation: The power method iteratively multiplies a vector by the matrix and normalizes, converging to the dominant eigenvalue. Works when one eigenvalue has largest absolute value.

title: Characteristic Polynomial Computation
description: Compute characteristic polynomial using Faddeev-Leverrier method
code:
FADDEEV_LEVERRIER(A):
 n = size of A
 p = [1] # coefficients list, p[0] = 1
 B = A
 
 for k in range(1, n+1):
 # Compute trace and update coefficients
 tr = trace(B) / k
 p.append(-tr)
 
 # Update B for next iteration
 B = A @ B + tr * I
 
 # p = [1, c₁, c₂, ..., cₙ] where char poly is λⁿ + c₁λⁿ⁻¹ + ... + cₙ
 return p

# Characteristic polynomial: det(A - λI) = 0
explanation: Faddeev-Leverrier computes characteristic polynomial coefficients recursively using matrix operations. Useful for symbolic computation of eigenvalues.
